channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: Dr. Raymond Jacobson Selected as Director of NIH Center for Scientific Review
item.description: National Institutes of Health (NIH) Director Jay Bhattacharya, M.D., announced the selection of Raymond H. Jacobson, Ph.D., as the director of NIH’s Center for Scientific Review (CSR), which ensures expert and fair review of the tens of thousands of grant applications received by the agency each year.

item.title: DARPA celebrates 20 years of Young Faculty Awards, announces Director’s Fellows
item.description: The YFA program has supported over 500 rising research stars from more than 60 institutions.

item.title: MXO Pitch Day
item.description: MXO will host an invitation-only, in-person Pitch Day on Nov. 2, 2026, in Chicago, Ill., in conjunction with the MXO Spark Tank event. | See Event

item.title: NIA to Sunset the Aged BALB/cBy and CB6F1 Mouse Colonies
item.description: Notice NOT-AG-26-009 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Notice of Change: Early Expiration of NINDS Ruth L. Kirschstein National Research Service Award (NRSA) for Training of Postdoctoral Fellows (F32 Clinical Trial Not Allowed)
item.description: Notice NOT-NS-26-002 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Plan Ahead with the NIH Grants and Funding Outreach Calendar
item.description: Looking for additional ways to keep up with updates on NIH wide programs/initiatives? The NIH Grants and Funding Calendar of Events could be one of the most useful tools you can keep on your radar.

item.title: DARPA THREADS the needle on thermal barriers to RF power
item.description: Program builds on its breakthrough performance gains to advance future operational capabilities.

item.title: Podcast: Bringing quantum concepts into classrooms
item.description: Erica Carlson and Muhsin Menekse discuss how their collaboration is inspiring the next generation of STEM workforce through a quantum education program...

item.title: NSF-supported teams advanced through the Presidential AI Challenge, with North Carolina teacher named national champion
item.description: A team supported by the U.S. National Science Foundation and sponsored by North Carolina State University emerged as a national champion of the inaugural...

item.title: Notice of Early Expiration of PAR-25-319 "Advancing HIV/AIDS Research within the Mission of the NIDCD (R01 Clinical Trial Optional)"
item.description: Notice NOT-DC-26-006 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Request for Information: Proposal to Cap the Number of Simultaneous Research Project Grants per Principal Investigator to Support More Researchers and Maximize Scientific Productivity and Innovation
item.description: Notice NOT-OD-26-086 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Feedback Sought: Proposal to Cap the Number of Simultaneous Research Project Grants per Principal Investigator to Support More Researchers and Maximize Scientific Productivity and Innovation
item.description: We are seeking your feedback on whether distributing Research Project Grants (RPGs) funding across more investigators could increase overall scientific productivity and innovation (see NOT-OD-26-086). Through this Request for Information (RFI), NIH is presenting a proposal as one possible approach to advance stewardship, workforce sustainability, and the goals of the NIH Unified Funding Strategy. Comments may be sent electronically here by August 3, 2026.

item.title: DARPA invites first wave of Lift Challenge competitors
item.description: First tranche of teams have been invited to compete for $6.5M in prizes.
